D5 Design L2 Understand Reference

Why RYB Isn't Science

mix the school-art primaries red-yellow-blue and the printer's cyan-magenta-yellow side by side — and watch which one muddies

"Red, yellow, blue are the primaries" is a studio tradition, not colour science. Subtractive mixing — paint absorbing light — is governed by cyan, magenta, yellow. Mix RYB and the secondaries come out dark and muddy, especially blue + yellow; mix CMY and they stay clean and bright.

Reveal the mixes. Each triangle's corners are its primaries; the edges are pairwise paint mixes and the centre is all three. Compare the green and purple each set produces — RYB cannot reach the vivid secondaries CMY gives.
